seo.title: Agentic Portfolio | Agentic Product Engineer
seo.description: Open-source agentic portfolio prototype for AI-assisted product development, production systems, security, and research.

hero.headline: Agentic Product Engineer building AI-assisted products from real-world systems experience.
hero.summary: A public prototype for product building with agents, production engineering discipline, open-source tools, and independent research.

sections.label: AGENTIC
sections.title: Agentic Product Engineering
sections.summary: Design and construction of autonomous systems that perceive, decide, and act to deliver real impact.

features.label: Practical Vision
features.description: Agents that turn intent into measurable results.

features.label: Verifiable Systems
features.description: Real constraints, observability, auditable decisions.

features.label: Design to Production
features.description: Fast prototypes to reliable, scalable solutions.

features.label: Real Impact
features.description: Value measurable in business and in people.

flow.label: SPECIFY
flow.description: Define objective, context, and verifiable constraints.

flow.label: PROTOTYPE
flow.description: Build fast, iterate with real data, validate assumptions.

flow.label: VERIFY
flow.description: Measure, test, ensure the agent meets its purpose.

flow.label: SHIP
flow.description: Deploy with monitoring, traceability, and continuous control.

detail.title: Agentic method
detail.summary: A way to build products with agents without losing direction: clear purpose, hard rules, verifiable prototype, and a path toward production.
points: The agent executes within defined technical and visual limits.
The prototype is validated as a real product, not as decoration.
Each iteration leaves traceable decisions for future development.

sections.label: PRODUCTS
sections.title: Public Products
sections.summary: Open-source tools and product experiments that demonstrate execution, safety, and usability.

features.label: Main Repository
features.description: The project that best shows current direction.

features.label: This Prototype
features.description: The site itself as evidence of the workflow.

features.label: Design Judgment
features.description: Choices made under real constraints, not on a whiteboard.

features.label: Maintainability
features.description: Code that survives past the demo.

flow.label: SCOPE
flow.description: Pick what is worth shipping and what is not.

flow.label: BUILD
flow.description: Working software over polished promises.

flow.label: HARDEN
flow.description: Tests, docs, error paths, observability.

flow.label: PUBLISH
flow.description: Open the artifact and own its evolution.

detail.title: Public products
detail.summary: The goal is public evidence of judgment: utility, design, maintenance, and visible evolution.
points: Select a small number of projects that support the current direction.
Each product should have concrete utility and inspectable code.
Documentation should explain both the result and the process.

links.label: Main repository
links.description: Replace this link with your most representative public project.

sections.label: SYSTEMS
sections.title: Production Systems
sections.summary: Legacy modernization, databases, architecture, cloud direction, and real operational constraints.

features.label: Legacy Modernization
features.description: Move forward without breaking what already works.

features.label: Data and Integrations
features.description: Schemas, contracts, and reliable boundaries.

features.label: Cloud Direction
features.description: A pragmatic path toward serverless and managed services.

features.label: Operational Continuity
features.description: Uptime, runbooks, and humane on-call.

flow.label: OBSERVE
flow.description: See the real system before changing it.

flow.label: CONTAIN
flow.description: Stabilize the riskiest seams first.

flow.label: EVOLVE
flow.description: Incremental rewrites under feature flags.

flow.label: OPERATE
flow.description: Logs, metrics, traces, and clear ownership.

detail.title: Production base
detail.summary: Agentic work needs engineering foundations: operational continuity, reliable data, clear boundaries, and reproducible deployments.
points: Observe the real system before automating it.
Separate boundaries so change can stay incremental.
Design with monitoring and recovery from the beginning.

sections.label: SECURITY
sections.title: Security Mindset
sections.summary: Secure defaults, MFA, rate limiting, validation, and production risk awareness.

features.label: Secure Defaults
features.description: Least privilege, no secrets in repos, signed releases.

features.label: Strong Auth
features.description: MFA, short-lived tokens, audit trail.

features.label: Input Validation
features.description: Schema-first inputs, rate limits, abuse controls.

features.label: Risk Proportion
features.description: Controls matched to actual blast radius.

flow.label: INVENTORY
flow.description: Know what you protect and why.

flow.label: HARDEN
flow.description: MFA, secrets, dependency hygiene, WAF where it pays.

flow.label: MONITOR
flow.description: Detect abuse, log carefully, avoid sensitive content in logs.

flow.label: RESPOND
flow.description: Rotate, contain, write a clean post-mortem.

detail.title: Proportional security
detail.summary: Security should respond to real risk: protect administration and sensitive data without creating unnecessary friction for visitors.
points: Use MFA for admin panels, not for public reading.
Add CAPTCHA or rate limits only when there is an abuse vector.
Secrets and private content should never live in the public repository.

sections.label: RESEARCH
sections.title: Independent Research
sections.summary: Work on AI, thought, truth, representation, papers, and long-form writing.

features.label: Theme: Thought
features.description: How representation shapes what a system can know.

features.label: Theme: Truth
features.description: Honest interfaces, faithful summaries, no hallucinated UX.

features.label: Theme: AI Use
features.description: Augmentation with auditability, not replacement.

features.label: Theme: Writing
features.description: Long-form drafts that hold up under their own claims.

flow.label: QUESTION
flow.description: Frame what is actually unknown.

flow.label: READ
flow.description: Build a small, well-cited prior map.

flow.label: DRAFT
flow.description: Argue in writing, not in slides.

flow.label: PUBLISH
flow.description: Curated, never a dump.

detail.title: Applied research
detail.summary: Research acts as conceptual depth: a way to think better about interfaces, systems, agents, and truth criteria.
points: Publish curated material, not full archives without context.
Connect abstract ideas to product decisions.
Use writing as a test of clarity and structure.

sections.label: CONTACT
sections.title: Contact
sections.summary: Professional links and contact channels for collaboration and opportunities.

features.label: Public Name
features.description: Use the searchable full name for record.

features.label: GitHub
features.description: Open-source work and this prototype.

features.label: LinkedIn
features.description: Professional context and history.

features.label: Email
features.description: Direct, low-noise channel for opportunities.

flow.label: OPEN
flow.description: Inbox is open for serious collaboration.

flow.label: BRIEF
flow.description: Send context: role, scope, constraints.

flow.label: MEET
flow.description: Short call to align on fit and scope.

flow.label: COMMIT
flow.description: Move only when both sides see the value.

detail.title: Professional channels
detail.summary: Contact should be direct and serious: public profiles for context, code for evidence, and a form for structured messages.
points: The form can add validation, CAPTCHA, or rate limits when needed.

links.label: GitHub
links.description: Replace this link with your public profile.
